response: This computes the **length of the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS)** between sequences `a` and `b` using classic **dynamic programming** with a `(m+1)×(n+1)` table. It fills `dp[i][j]` so that when characters match it takes `dp[i-1][j-1] + 1`, otherwise it takes the maximum of `dp[i-1][j]` and `dp[i][j-1]`.
